reasoning_model_1: Model 1 failed significantly on Content Accuracy. The user requested the model to read the specific sentence: '请你仔细听，这个步骤很重要。'. However, the model hallucinated a large amount of additional text ('我慢慢说，一字一句不着急...' etc.), extending the audio to 14 seconds. Regarding the '语速' (Speaking Rate) dimension, the pacing of the initial target sentence (00:00-00:02) was moderate/conversational, not distinctly 'slow' as requested. The subsequent hallucinated text was also spoken at a standard conversational speed. Due to the major content violation and the failure to implement the specific 'slow' paralinguistic feature, this generation is rated poorly.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 followed the instructions perfectly. Content Accuracy is 3/3; it read exactly the requested text '请你仔细听，这个步骤很重要' without any additions or omissions. Regarding the '语速' (Speaking Rate) dimension, the model successfully adopted a 'slow' pace. It included deliberate pauses between phrases (e.g., at 00:01 after '听', and 00:03 before '很重要') and elongated the pronunciation of characters to lower the words-per-minute rate. This effectively conveys the importance of the step through a measured, slow tempo as requested.
model_comparison: Model 1 completely failed the content constraints by generating a long, unrequested monologue, and its speaking rate was standard rather than slow. Model 2 adhered strictly to the script and correctly applied the 'slow' speaking rate feature with appropriate pauses and pacing. Therefore, Model 2 is the clear winner.
